data.title: As a developer, sprint uses ast-grep for batch overlap context when available
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add ast-grep guidance to the sprint skill's Phase 3 (Batch Preparation) so agents use sg for structural pattern matching when analyzing file overlap between batch candidates and discovering cross-file dependencies.
**Why**: Batch overlap analysis determines whether sub-agents will conflict. Structural search finds actual code dependencies rather than string matches, reducing false negatives in overlap detection.
**Scope**:
- IN: sprint SKILL.md Phase 3 update with sg command examples for dependency-aware overlap analysis, command -v sg guard, Grep fallback instruction
- OUT: Changes to other skills, changes to sprint-next-batch.sh script logic, ast-grep pattern config files

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, sprint SKILL.md Phase 3 (Batch Preparation) contains ast-grep guidance with a command -v sg guard and Grep fallback
  ← Satisfies: "Skills reference ast-grep in specific dependency discovery steps: sprint Phase 3"
- When this story is complete, running sprint on a system without ast-grep installed produces no errors and falls back to Grep-based search
  ← Satisfies: "Graceful degradation"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Sprint SKILL.md is large — keep the addition minimal and well-placed within the existing Phase 3 flow
